response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend developer with a strong track record in web, TypeScript, and distributed systems, but he is a poor fit for this role on multiple critical dimensions. Most disqualifying is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard native-level requirement. Beyond that, his resume shows no security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal, encryption protocols), no embedded networking or LAN-switch development experience, and only marginal C/C++ work (a single NI role and a thesis project). The core technical stack this role demands—embedded C on Linux with security feature ownership—is not represented in his work history.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems demonstrates adaptability and seniority
Has worked with C and C++ at National Instruments and on a Linux-based thesis project, showing some foundational exposure to systems-level programming
Familiarity with Linux, REST APIs, and open-source development workflows aligns with peripheral tooling mentioned in the job description
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed anywhere—this is a stated hard requirement for the role and is an automatic disqualifier
Zero demonstrable experience in network security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, captive portal, encryption key exchange) that form the core of the job
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, Linux kernel modification, or chip-vendor SDK experience; career is firmly in web/cloud/backend development
